# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# Deployment Manager template for GCE instances
# NOTE: This is a quick-start template. Phase 2 should add:
#   - Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) for disks
#   - Custom service accounts with least-privilege IAM roles
#   - VPC Service Controls and firewall hardening
#   - Shielded VM settings and OS Login enforcement

imports:
- path: vm-template.jinja

resources:

# --- Web Server Instance ---
- name: web-server-instance
  type: vm-template.jinja
  properties:
    instanceName: web-server-01
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: e2-medium
    network: default
    subnet: default
    sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-12
    diskSizeGb: 50
    diskType: pd-balanced
    tags:
      - http-server
      - https-server
    labels:
      environment: development
      team: web
      phase: quick-deploy
    metadata:
      startup-script: |
        #!/bin/bash
        apt-get update
        apt-get install -y nginx
        systemctl enable nginx
        systemctl start nginx

# --- Application Server Instance ---
- name: app-server-instance
  type: vm-template.jinja
  properties:
    instanceName: app-server-01
    zone: us-central1-b
    machineType: e2-standard-2
    network: default
    subnet: default
    sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-12
    diskSizeGb: 100
    diskType: pd-ssd
    tags:
      - app-server
    labels:
      environment: development
      team: backend
      phase: quick-deploy
    metadata:
      startup-script: |
        #!/bin/bash
        apt-get update
        apt-get install -y default-jdk

outputs:
- name: webServerSelfLink
  value: $(ref.web-server-instance.selfLink)
- name: webServerInternalIP
  value: $(ref.web-server-instance.internalIP)
- name: appServerSelfLink
  value: $(ref.app-server-instance.selfLink)
- name: appServerInternalIP
  value: $(ref.app-server-instance.internalIP)

# Save the following as vm-template.jinja in the same directory:
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# File: vm-template.jinja
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# {% set instanceName = properties['instanceName'] %}
# {% set zone = properties['zone'] %}
# {% set machineType = properties['machineType'] %}
# {% set network = properties['network'] %}
# {% set subnet = properties['subnet'] %}
# {% set sourceImage = properties['sourceImage'] %}
# {% set diskSizeGb = properties['diskSizeGb'] %}
# {% set diskType = properties['diskType'] %}
# {% set tags = properties['tags'] %}
# {% set labels = properties['labels'] %}
# {% set metadata = properties.get('metadata', {}) %}
#
# resources:
# - name: {{ instanceName }}
#   type: compute.v1.instance
#   properties:
#     zone: {{ zone }}
#     machineType: zones/{{ zone }}/machineTypes/{{ machineType }}
#     # TODO Phase 2: Replace default service account with a dedicated
#     # service account following the principle of least privilege.
#     # Current config uses the Compute Engine default service account
#     # with full cloud-platform scope which is overly permissive.
#     serviceAccounts:
#       - email: default
#         scopes:
#           - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform
#     tags:
#       items:
#       {% for tag in tags %}
#         - {{ tag }}
#       {% endfor %}
#     labels:
#       {% for key, value in labels.items() %}
#       {{ key }}: "{{ value }}"
#       {% endfor %}
#     disks:
#       - deviceName: boot-disk
#         type: PERSISTENT
#         boot: true
#         autoDelete: true
#         initializeParams:
#           sourceImage: {{ sourceImage }}
#           diskSizeGb: {{ diskSizeGb }}
#           diskType: zones/{{ zone }}/diskTypes/{{ diskType }}
#         # TODO Phase 2: Add diskEncryptionKey with customer-managed
#         # encryption key (CMEK) from Cloud KMS:
#         # diskEncryptionKey:
#         #   kmsKeyName: projects/PROJECT/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/KEYRING/cryptoKeys/KEY
#     networkInterfaces:
#       - network: global/networks/{{ network }}
#         subnetwork: regions/{{ zone[:-2] }}/subnetworks/{{ subnet }}
#         accessConfigs:
#           - name: External NAT
#             type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
#     {% if metadata %}
#     metadata:
#       items:
#       {% for key, value in metadata.items() %}
#         - key: {{ key }}
#           value: |
#             {{ value | indent(12) }}
#       {% endfor %}
#     {% endif %}
#     # TODO Phase 2: Enable Shielded VM features
#     # shieldedInstanceConfig:
#     #   enableSecureBoot: true
#     #   enableVtpm: true
#     #   enableIntegrityMonitoring: true
#
# outputs:
# - name: selfLink
#   value: $(ref.{{ instanceName }}.selfLink)
# - name: internalIP
#   value: $(ref.{{ instanceName }}.networkInterfaces[0].networkIP)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
